name: Cocoa
description: Cocoa is the concentrated base ingredient of all chocolate products, produced by processing cacao seeds. It is the most potent source of methylxanthines — theobromine and caffeine — of any common household food product. Dry cocoa powder contains approximately 30 mg of methylxanthines per gram, making it significantly more dangerous weight-for-weight than finished chocolate. Cats metabolise methylxanthines far more slowly than humans: these compounds block adenosine receptors, inhibit phosphodiesterase, and disrupt calcium signalling in cardiac and skeletal muscles, causing a cascade of CNS and cardiovascular effects.
safetyNotes: Symptoms: Vomiting, diarrhea, excessive thirst and urination; rapidly progressing to agitation, rapid heart rate, tremors, hyperthermia, cardiac arrhythmia, and seizures.
Most toxic common source: Cocoa powder is more dangerous per gram than dark chocolate, baker's chocolate, or any other chocolate product — treat any exposure as a serious emergency.
Hidden sources: Hot cocoa powder, chocolate baking mixes, and cocoa mulch (used in gardens) all contain high methylxanthine levels.
Action: Veterinary emergency. Even a small amount of cocoa powder requires immediate veterinary attention.

symptoms.name: Gastrointestinal Upset (Vomiting and Diarrhea)
symptoms.onset: 2 to 12 hours (often the initial symptoms to manifest).
symptoms.notes: These are generally the first clinical signs of methylxanthine (theobromine and caffeine) poisoning after a cat consumes cocoa products

symptoms.name: Increased Thirst and Urination (Polydipsia and Polyuria)
symptoms.notes: Theobromine acts as a diuretic, increasing the loss of bodily fluids and causing the cat to pass large amounts of urine, which can rapidly lead to systemic dehydration

symptoms.name: Severe Neurological Overstimulation
symptoms.onset: 2 to 12 hours (progresses as the toxins are absorbed into the bloodstream).
symptoms.notes: Methylxanthines cause severe central nervous system overstimulation. The cat may initially show disorientation and extreme agitation, which can rapidly progress to severe muscle tremors, convulsions, and potentially a coma

symptoms.name: Cardiovascular Dysfunction
symptoms.notes: Theobromine and caffeine abnormally enhance the strength and contractility of cardiac muscles. This results in very rapid, irregular heart rhythms, potential internal bleeding, and ultimately cardiac arrest or a heart attack in the terminal phase

symptoms.name: Respiratory Distress
symptoms.onset: Mid to late stages (progressing from 6 to 12 hours).
symptoms.notes: As cardiovascular and systemic distress worsens, cats will pant and breathe rapidly2.... They may develop a bluish tinge to their skin and mucous membranes (cyanosis) due to poor oxygenation, culminating in respiratory failure

symptoms.name: Hyperthermia
symptoms.onset: Mid to late stage (6 to 12 hours).
symptoms.notes: The extreme overstimulation of the nervous system and relentless muscle tremors generate excessive heat, causing the cat's core body temperature to spike to dangerous levels
